title: Molecular Diagnosis of Viral Hepatitis B Infection
author: Frank Twum Aboagye

steps.title: Transfer 200 µL of Sample to a sterile 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 200 µL of Sample to a sterile 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube (pre-labeled).

steps.title: Add 5 µL of Internal Control...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of Internal Control (provided with the amplification kit) to each specimen and vortex for 00:00:30

steps.title: Add 400 µL of Genomic Lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of Genomic Lysis Buffer and 10 µL of Proteinase K to the sample.

steps.title: Vortex and incubate the sample at...
steps.instruction: Vortex and incubate the sample at 56 °C for 3 - 5 hours or Overnight .

steps.title: Vortex the sample at 3000 rpm, °C, for 00:00:30
steps.instruction: Vortex the sample at 3000 rpm, °C, for 00:00:30

steps.title: Transfer the entire content of the 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire content of the 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube into a Zymo-Spin IIC Column in a collection tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10000 rpm, , for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10000 rpm, , for 00:01:00 . Discard the flow-through liquid.

steps.title: Transfer the Zymo-Spin IIC column into a new collection tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the Zymo-Spin IIC column into a new collection tube.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of DNA Pre-Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of DNA Pre-Wash Buffer to the spin column and centrifuge 10000 rpm, , for 00:01:00 . Discard the flow-through liquid.
10. Add 500 µL of gDNA Wash Buffer to the spin column and centrifuge at 10000 rpm, , for 00:01:00 . Discard the flow-through liquid.
11. Transfer the Zymo-Spin IIC Column into a sterile 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube
12. Add 100 µL, (70 - 100) of DNA Elution Buffer to the spin column and incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00
13. Centrifuge at 13000 rpm, , for 00:00:30 to elute the DNA.
14. Store the Viral Nucleic acid at -20 °C pending further analysis.

steps.title: The PCR is done in a...
steps.instruction: The PCR is done in a 25 µL reaction as described below: A B 1 Reagent/Component X1 (µL) 2 PCR Master mix 15 3 Test Nucleic acid/Standards/Negative Control/Positive Control 10 4 Total Reaction Volume 25 µL Allow the PCR Master Mix and the other components of the Bosphore HBV Quantification kit to thaw completely at 4°C before use and avoid centrifuging to thaw.

steps.title: The reaction is done under the following cycling conditions
steps.instruction: The reaction is done under the following cycling conditions: A B C D 1 Step Temperature (°C) Time (minutes) Cycles 2 Initial Denaturation 95 14:30 NA 3 Denaturation 97 00:30 50 cycles 4 Annealing and Synthesis (Data Collection) 54 01:30 5 Hold 32 01:00 NA

steps.title: Select the appropriate fluorophore depending on the analyzer been used
steps.instruction: Select the appropriate fluorophore depending on the analyzer been used. For Bio-Rad CFX 1000 Series, select the FAM dye for HBV and HEX for Internal Control (IC). FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX FAM and HEX

steps.title: Samples that cross the threshold in...
steps.instruction: Samples that cross the threshold in the FAM channel are displayed with their starting quantities and the corresponding Ct-values. Samples that do not cross the threshold are displayed as “No Ct” or “ –”

steps.title: Possible outcomes from the PCR
steps.instruction: Possible outcomes from the PCR: A B C D 1 + - HBV Positive The sample contains a high viral load of HBV and may suppress the amplification of the Internal Control. No need to check the HEX 2 + + HBV Positive The sample contains HBV Nucleic acid 3 + - HBV Positive* Caution: If the sample has a viral load 4 - + HBV Negative No HBV Nucleic acid was detected. Internal control amplified indicating good DNA isolation and PCR procedures. 5 - - Invalid** Repeat the assay (see troubleshooting) * and **: refer to the troubleshooting section

steps.title: Initiate the protocol
steps.instruction: Initiate the protocol

steps.title: *If the sample has a viral...
steps.instruction: *If the sample has a viral load and no Internal Control amplification, it requires that the assay is repeated once. Freeze-thaw[freeze the sample (temperature ≤ -10°C) for 20 minutes and defrost at room temperature for 10 minutes] the nucleic acid sample and dilute it with nuclease-free water in a ratio of 1:2. Use the diluted nucleic acid for repeating the assay ** Repeat the assay once taking into consideration possible pipetting errors in the first assay. If both repeat as indicated above fail to yield the expected/better results, a new specimen should be collected from the patient and the process from nucleic acid isolation to amplification should be repeated with the new sample.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of β-mercaptoethanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of β-mercaptoethanol to 100 mL of Genomic Lysis Buffer Cap the bottle tightly after each use

steps.title: Identify unknown samples (test samples), standards,...
steps.instruction: Identify unknown samples (test samples), standards, positive control, and negative control on the plate as described below. Assign the right quantitative values to the standards Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Negative Control Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Standard 1 Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Standard 2 Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Standard 3 Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Standard 4 Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Unknown Positive Control
